data.title: PubMatic expects Q1 revenue of $62.M; shares slide 12%
data.description:
• PubMatic (PUBM) on Wednesday reported preliminary unaudited first quarter 2026 revenue to be approximately $62.4M vs. consensus of $58.95M, with approximately $2.5 million in adjusted EBITDA. 
  • The company said that these figures exceed its previously issued first quarter 2026 guidance of $58M to $60M in revenue and $(0.5M) to $1M in adjusted EBITDA. 
  • However, the company's shares slipped nearly 12% in extended trading on Wednesday. 
  • The company said that Paulina Klimenko, chief growth officer, is stepping down to focus on her health. Chief Revenue Officer for the Americas Kyle Dozeman is departing to pursue an entrepreneurship opportunity. 
  • Paulina Klimenko will remain in her role through July 2026 to ensure a structured transition. Kyle Dozeman will remain with PubMatic through the end of May and continue in an advisory capacity thereafter, it added. 
  • PubMatic has retained Heidrick & Struggles to conduct a search for a global chief revenue officer. The company intends to consolidate all revenue-generating functions under this global chief revenue officer, positioning the organization for accelerated, long-term profitable growth across the business.
  • The company highlighted that Chief Revenue Officers Emma Newman (EMEA) and Jason Barnes (APAC) will continue to lead their respective regions.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Graco shares slide after quarterly misses, underlying weakness
data.description:
Graco (GGG), a manufacturer of fluid-handling systems and spray equipment used in construction, manufacturing and maintenance markets, reported results on Wednesday that missed Wall Street expectations on both revenue and earnings.

Shares of Graco (GGG) fell 4.2% in extended trading. At Wednesday’s close, the stock was up 8.3% over the prior 12 months.

Revenue for the first quarter ended March 27 rose 2% to $540.1 million from $528.3 million a year earlier, missing analysts’ consensus estimate of $560.7 million. Adjusted diluted earnings per share were $0.66, down from $0.70 a year earlier and below estimates of $0.74 a share.

Net income fell to $118.5 million, or $0.70 a share, from $124.1 million, or $0.72 a share, a year earlier.

Acquisitions mask organic weakness

The company said sales growth was driven by acquisitions and favorable currency translation, which together added eight percentage points of growth. That more than offset a 6 percentage point decline in organic sales. Organic growth is the increase in a company’s sales or profit from its existing businesses through higher demand, pricing, new products or market share gains, excluding boosts from acquisitions, currency changes or one-time factors.

“Sales increased 2 percent in the quarter, with contributions from acquired businesses offsetting organic revenue declines,” Chief Executive Mark Sheahan said in the earnings release.

Operating earnings fell 4% to $137.8 million as lower gross margins and higher expenses weighed on results. Gross margin pressure stemmed from unfavorable product mix, lower-margin acquired operations and tariff costs, though pricing actions offset much of the tariff impact.

Contractor unit holds up, housing still soft

Graco (GGG) said its contractor segment, which includes paint sprayers and pavement-marking systems, posted 2% sales growth as infrastructure demand remained solid. Residential construction activity, however, stayed soft.

The industrial segment grew 4%, helped by acquisitions, though profitability declined because of project timing in powder finishing systems and weaker mix. Expansion markets sales fell 4%, led by lower semiconductor-related demand in the Americas.

Full-year outlook maintained

Despite the slow start, Graco (GGG) maintained its 2026 outlook for low single-digit organic revenue growth on a constant-currency basis and mid-single-digit total growth including acquisitions.

The company said order backlog in organic businesses increased 13% from the end of 2025, suggesting customer demand improved as the quarter progressed.

Graco (GGG) is known for products such as paint sprayers, coating systems, lubrication equipment and dispensing pumps sold under the Graco brand, with strong positions in contractor, industrial finishing and fluid management markets. Its spray systems are widely used by professional painters and manufacturers.

data.title: DelphX announces non-brokered private placement
data.description:
• DelphX Capital Markets (DELX:CA) (DPXCF) on Wednesday said it intends to proceed with a non-brokered private placement of up to 3.75M units at a subscription price of C$0.04 per unit, for gross proceeds of up to C$150,000.
  • Each unit will consist of one common share and one common share purchase warrant. Each warrant will entitle the holder to purchase one common share at a price of C$0.08 for a period of two years from the date of issuance.
  • DelphX intends to use the net proceeds from the offering in connection with general corporate purposes.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: QuantumScape sees increased demand outside of auto industry
data.description:
Shares of QuantumScape (QS) are rallying after-hours as the company improved its profitability and revealed increased interest in its battery technology from sectors outside the auto industry.

In a letter to shareholders, QuantumScape (QS) said it is “ramping up engagements in new markets” as its high-performance solid-state design has generated “strong customer interest from global players in the military, aerospace, and government sectors.“

In addition, “in-rack energy storage and power delivery for AI data centers is a large and fast-growing market, and the higher energy density of our battery technology can enable increased compute density of AI data centers.”

The company also noted the “substantial progress” on cell progress and reliability for its Eagle Line production line to enable increased production of QSE-5 cell production for the automotive industry.

“We believe that the increased capacity of the Eagle Line will help drive a virtuous cycle of higher data volume, more rapid learning cycles, and increasing production quality.”

For the reported quarter, QuantumScape (QS) reported a loss of $0.16 per share, an improvement from a loss of $0.21 per share in the same quarter last year and a penny better than expected. Adjusted EBITDA improved from a loss of $64.6M to a loss of $63.2M.

Shares are up more than 17% in after-hours trading.

data.title: Greenwich LifeSciences receives Nasdaq notice regarding late Form 10-K filing
data.description:
• Greenwich LifeSciences (GLSI) on Wednesday announced that it has received notification from Nasdaq, notifying the company that, because it has not yet filed its Annual Report on Form 10-K for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2025, it is not currently in compliance with listing rules.
  • The notice has no immediate effect on the listing or trading of the company's common stock, which continues to trade on the Nasdaq Capital Market under the symbol "GLSI."
  • The company plans to file its Form 10-K for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2025 as soon as possible.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Oak Valley Bancorp GAAP EPS of $0.64, revenue of $20.78M

data.title: Oceaneering Non-GAAP EPS of $0.30 misses by $0.03, revenue of $692M beats by $20.65M
data.description:
• Oceaneering press release (OII): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.30 misses by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $692M (+2.6% Y/Y) beats by $20.65M.
    • Operating income was $57.8 million, a decrease of 21%.
    • Net income was $36.1 million, a decrease of 28%.
    • Adjusted EBITDA was $83.7 million, a decrease of 13%.
  • Cash Flow
    • Cash flow used in operating activities was $(59.1) million.
    • Free cash flow was $(76.5) million.
    • Quarter-end cash and cash equivalents totaled $607 million, compared to $382 million at the end of the same period last year.

data.title: Fulton Financial GAAP EPS of $0.51 beats by $0.04, revenue of $331.8M misses by $2.28M
data.description:
• Fulton Financial press release (FULT): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.51 beats by $0.04.
  • Revenue of $331.8M (+4.2% Y/Y) misses by $2.28M.

data.title: Astra Exploration upsizes private placement to C$13M
data.description:
• Astra Exploration (ASTR:CA) (OTCQB: ATEPF) on Wednesday said it has increased the size of its bought deal private placement to about C$13 million from a previously announced offering.
  • The company will issue about 16.9 million shares at C$0.77 each, and has granted underwriters an option to purchase an additional roughly 2.6 million shares, which could raise total proceeds to about C$15 million if fully exercised.
  • Astra said it plans to use the proceeds for exploration and development of its La Manchuria project, as well as for general corporate and working capital purposes.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about April 30, subject to regulatory approvals, including from the TSX Venture Exchange.
  • ASTR:CA closed +1.19% at $0.85.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: ISS says ConocoPhillips shareholders should vote for independent board chair
data.description:
Proxy advisor Institutional Shareholder Services said Wednesday that ConocoPhillips (COP) shareholders should vote in favor of a proposal calling for an independent board chair, citing ​the need for stronger oversight of management.

ISS said it backs the non-binding proposal that asks Conoco (COP) to separate the roles of chair and CEO, with an independent director holding the board chair position, citing concerns over the current lead director role, which is chosen ​by non-employee directors and not independent directors.

"The board is responsible for ​overseeing management and instilling accountability, and conflicts of interest may arise when ​one person holds both the chairman and CEO positions," ISS said.

ConocoPhillips' (COP) governance structure is similar to that of Exxon Mobil and Chevron, where the CEOs ⁠also ​serve as chairmen on their respective boards; BP has separate CEO and chairman positions.

Conoco's (COP) board has recommended ​that shareholders ⁠vote against the proposal for an independent board chairman; Ryan Lance currently serves ‌as ⁠both Chairman and CEO.
